english: 1. for tem (them), full Glr. — 2. for tabs (thabs) Glr., C. — 3. téb-mo (theb mo), teb-čén (theb chen) the thumb, teb-čúṅ (theb chung) the little finger; v. mte-boṅ (mthe bong).
tebs (thebs) series, order, succession Sch., tebs-re byed-pa (thebs re byed pa) to do successively; tébs-pa (thebs pa) v. _tebs-pa ('thebs pa).

I. 1. threshold, rgál-ba (rgal ba) to cross it Glr.; sgo-tém (sgo them) door-sill, threshold; yá-tém (ya them) head-piece of a door-frame, lintel, ma-tém (ma them) sill, threshold Glr. — 2. staircaise, stairs, flight of steps, tem-skás (them skas) id.; *tem-só* (*them so*) W. step, stair; tem-rím (them rim) Cs. 1. the several steps of a staircase. 2. rank, dignity. — rdo-tém (rdo them) stone staircase; _kor-tém ('khor them) winding stairs Cs. — II. 1. to be full, complete, zla-dus tem-pa daṅ (zla dus them pa dang) when the time of the months was fulfilled Glr. frq.; żag yčig ma tém-pa-la (zhag gcig ma them pa la) one day being still wanting Glr.; brgya tém-pa (brgya them pa) v. tam-pa (tham pa) Glr. — 2. W.: to be sufficient, enough. — 3. to receive (?) Sch. III. Sch. = tén-pa (then pa), tax, impost, tribute.
tém-bu (them bu), tem-tsaṅs (them tshangs) stopping, closing, shutting up; a stoppage Sch.
tems-yig (thems yig) Sch. memorial.
teu-ráṅ (the'u rang) Glr., te-bráṅ (the brang) Lit., te-ráṅ (the rang) Ma., a sort of demons.

1. bald, bare, spyi-tér (spyi ther) Thgy. a bald head; a bald-headed person; ter-tér (ther ther) C. flat. — 2. = te-ré (?) (the re (?)) _pyi ter naṅ gog ('phyi ther nang gog) strong and hale outside, decayed within Mil.; ter-zúg-pa (ther zug pa) = rtág-pa (rtag pa) Thgr.
ter-búm (ther 'bum) Sch. 1000 000 000; ter-_bum-čén-po (ther 'bum chen po) 10 000 000 000.
a kind of thin woollen cloth, a flannel-like fabric, le-ter (le ther) made of shawl-wool, bal-ter (bal ther) of common wool.
tel (thel) for te-li (the li), rag-tél (rag thel) C. a plate made of latten brass.
W. frq. = sléb-pa (sleb pa) to arrive, cf. tál-ba (thal ba) II., 4.
tél-se (thel se) Sch. and Wts. a seal, stamp, = te-tse (the tse).
tés-pa (thes pa) Sch. pf. to té-ba (the ba); = tes-bsún (thes bsun) Lt.?

1. num. for 130. — 2. register, list, catalogue, index; to _bri-ba (tho 'bri ba) to register, to make out a list or catalogue Schr.; sléb-to (sleb tho), _byúṅ-to ('byung tho) account of receipts, sóṅ-to (song tho), búd-to (bud tho), skyág-to (skyag tho) account of expenditures; btáṅ-to (btang tho) account of money or goods lent out; nyó-to (nyo tho) account of goods bought, bill; lo-to (lo tho) calendar, almanac; dei lág-tu _prin-bor-to (de'i lag tu 'phrin bor tho) list of orders or directions given to him (lit. laid down in his hands); dei rgyúd-la tób-to (de'i rgyud la thob tho) a list of things which his relations shall receive.
to-gár (tho gar) Pth.; acc. to Sch. the Turkomans; Tar. 18, Schf.: Tukhara, name of a people in the northwest of India; prob. the Togarmah of the Bible.
to-čo (tho co) Mil., a foolish joke, unbefitting a sensible man.
to-pyi (tho phyi) Schr. love(?), in Pth. it seems to signify the sky.

a large hammer, tó-bas rdúṅ-ba (tho bas rdung ba) to hammer, to forge; rdó-to (rdo tho) a stone hammer, šiṅ-to (shing tho) a wooden hammer, mallet; *to-čúṅ* (*tho chung*) 1. an ordinary hammer. — 2. the cock of a gun. — 3. a soldering-stick. Lh.

to-_tsám-pa (tho 'tsham pa) to scorn, scoff, jeer, sneer at, vex, insult, mock, c. la, by words Dzl., also by actions Dzl.; sñan-čud to-_tsám-pa bzód-par gsol (snyan cud tho 'tsham pa bzod par gsol) pardon our having sneered at you before! Mil.; also mto-mtsám-pa (mtho mtsham pa), -btsám-pa (-btsham pa), -brtsám-pa (-brtsham pa).
to-yór (tho yor) stone pyramid, heap of stones (cairn).

1. dawn, break of day, early morning, to-ráṅs(-kyi) dús-su (tho rangs(-kyi) dus su) early in the morning; 2. the following, the next morning, c. genit.; both also adverbially: de daṅ mjál-bai to-ráṅs (de dang mjal ba'i tho rangs) on the morning after having met him.
W. to-morrow (B., C. saṅ (sang)).
Cs.: a few; Mil., tog-re-tsal (thog re tshal) a little while.
v. tu-lúm (thu lum).
1. to-lé _débs-pa (tho le 'debs pa) to spit, c. la, at or on Pth. (cf. tu (thu)). — 2. button C. — 3. to-lé dkár-po (tho le dkar po) C. chalk. — 4. to-le-rgyal (tho le rgyal) Mil.?
